sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on the sky blue structure
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation

prev_colors = {}
for n in prev_seg['nodes']:
    prev_colors[n['color']] = prev_colors.get(n['color'], 0) + 1

curr_colors = {}
for n in curr_seg['nodes']:
    curr_colors[n['color']] = curr_colors.get(n['color'], 0) + 1

print(f"Previous colors: {prev_colors}")
print(f"Current colors: {curr_colors}")

# Check if the sky blue structure changed
prev_sky = [n for n in prev_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
curr_sky = [n for n in curr_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
print(f"Previous sky blue: {len(prev_sky)}, pixels: {[n['pixels'] for n in prev_sky]}")
print(f"Current sky blue: {len(curr_sky)}, pixels: {[n['pixels'] for n in curr_sky]}")

# Check if any node changed hash
prev_hashes = {n['hash']: n['color'] for n in prev_seg['nodes']}
curr_hashes = {n['hash']: n['color'] for n in curr_seg['nodes']}
print(f"Previous hashes: {prev_hashes}")
print(f"Current hashes: {curr_hashes}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check what changed between the initial state and after converting green blocks
# to understand if dark gray dots disappeared

init_seg = history[0].frame.segmentation
init_green = [n for n in init_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
init_gray = [n for n in init_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"Initial green: {len(init_green)}, gray: {len(init_gray)}")

# Check after converting all green blocks (entry 10)
after_green_seg = history[10].frame.segmentation
after_green_gray = [n for n in after_green_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"After green conversion: gray: {len(after_green_gray)}")

# Check current state
curr_gray = [n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"Current gray: {len(curr_gray)}")

# Check if gray dots changed between after-green and current
print(f"Gray dots changed: {len(after_green_gray) != len(curr_gray)}")

# Let me also check the initial state more carefully
# Maybe there were other objects I missed
init_colors = {}
for n in init_seg['nodes']:
    init_colors[n['color']] = init_colors.get(n['color'], 0) + 1
print(f"Initial colors: {init_colors}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
